title: Pork-and-Apple Bobotie
excerpt: Ingredients Meat layer 1 large, finely chopped onion 2 large, finely grated garlic cloves 30 ml butter 30 ml sunflower oil 1 kg pork mince 45 ml medium curry powder 15 ml turmeric 7.5 ml salt 5 ml white pepper 5 ml ground cumin 2.5 ml nutmeg 2.5 ml ground cloves 1 ml allspice 2 …
html:
Ingredients

Meat layer

  • 1 large, finely chopped onion
  • 2 large, finely grated garlic cloves
  • 30 ml butter
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 1 kg pork mince
  • 45 ml medium curry powder
  • 15 ml turmeric
  • 7.5 ml salt
  • 5 ml white pepper
  • 5 ml ground cumin
  • 2.5 ml nutmeg
  • 2.5 ml ground cloves
  • 1 ml allspice
  • 2 large apples, peeled and cut into small cubes (about 375 ml)
  • 180 ml seedless sultanas
  • 125 ml water
  • 250 ml milk
  • 2 jumbo eggs
  • 65 ml chutney
  • 45 ml cornflour
  • 30 ml white vinegar
  • 2 thick slices of bread, crusts removed and cut into cubes

Custard topping

  • 375 ml milk
  • 3 jumbo eggs
  • 2.5 ml salt
  • 5 ml turmeric
  • 65 ml chutney
  • 65 ml finely chopped coriander leaves
  • finely grated zest of one lemon
  • 6 lemon or bay leaves

Method

For the meat layer, lightly fry the onion and garlic in the butter and oil.

Add the meat and fry until lightly browned.

Add the curry powder, turmeric, salt, white pepper, cumin, nutmeg, cloves and allspice and fry until fragrant.

Add the apples, sultanas and water.

Lower the heat and let the mixture simmer for 15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced.

Stir occasionally.

Whisk together the milk, eggs, chutney, cornflour and vinegar and pour over the bread.

Let it soak for a few minutes and then mix thoroughly with the meat.

Spoon the mixture into an oven dish and bake at 190°C for 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, prepare the custard topping: whisk together all the ingredients, except the lemon or bay leaves.

Remove the meat from the oven and carefully pour the custard mixture over the top.

Tuck in the leaves and bake further in the oven at 180°C for 20 – 25 minutes or until the bobotie is puffed and cooked through.

Remove from the oven and let the bobotie rest for 15 minutes.

Serve 8 generous portions with yellow rice and tomato-and-cucumber sambals.

Easy!
